children.name: RegisterPatient
children.purpose: Register a new patient in the system
children.behavior: Extract patient details from input, validate uniqueness, and store in patients data store
postconditions: patient record created in patients data store

inputs.name: patient_data
inputs.description: Patient details including name, age, contact, medical_history

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, patient_id, and message

global_vars.description: Write new patient record to patients data store

constraints.description: Patient ID must be unique across the system
Patient data must include all required fields (name, age, contact)

acceptance_criteria.description: Given valid patient data, when RegisterPatient is called, then a new patient record is created and a unique patient_id is returned
Given duplicate patient data, when RegisterPatient is called, then an error is returned and no duplicate record is created

children.name: BookAppointment
children.purpose: Book an appointment for a registered patient
children.behavior: Validate patient exists, check appointment slot availability, and store appointment in appointments data store
postconditions: appointment record created in appointments data store

inputs.name: appointment_data
inputs.description: Appointment details including patient_id, doctor, date, time

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, appointment_id, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence

constraints.description: Appointment slot must not be double-booked
Patient must be registered before booking

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient and available slot, when BookAppointment is called, then an appointment is created and appointment_id is returned
Given an unregistered patient, when BookAppointment is called, then an error is returned
Given an already booked slot, when BookAppointment is called, then an error is returned

children.name: GetRecords
children.purpose: Retrieve medical records for a registered patient
children.behavior: Validate patient exists, fetch records from patients data store (append-only), and return them

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, records list, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record and associated medical records

constraints.description: Patient must be registered to retrieve records

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient, when GetRecords is called, then the patient's medical records are returned
Given an unregistered patient, when GetRecords is called, then an error is returned

children.name: UpdateProfile
children.purpose: Update profile information for a registered patient
children.behavior: Validate patient exists, update patient profile fields in patients data store
postconditions: patient profile updated in patients data store

inputs.name: update_data
inputs.description: JSON with patient_id and fields to update (e.g., contact, address)

outputs.name: result
outputs.description: JSON with success status, updated profile, and message

global_vars.description: Read patient record to verify existence, then update profile fields

data_operations.description: Read patient record, then update specified profile fields

constraints.description: Only specified fields are updated, others remain unchanged
Patient must be registered to update profile

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a registered patient and valid update data, when UpdateProfile is called, then the patient profile is updated and the updated profile is returned
Given an unregistered patient, when UpdateProfile is called, then an error is returned
Given invalid fields in update data, when UpdateProfile is called, then an error is returned and no changes are made

governance_notes: All children operate on the patients data store. RegisterPatient and UpdateProfile require write access to patients. BookAppointment requires read access to patients and write access to appointments. GetRecords requires only read access to patients. No child requires access to both data stores simultaneously. The parent should route commands based on the command field in the input JSON.
